clusters.title: Trump Fires Attorney General Pam Bondi Amid Epstein Controversy
clusters.summary: President Trump has dismissed Attorney General Pam Bondi following mounting criticism regarding her handling of documents related to Jeffrey Epstein. Bondi's tenure was marked by scrutiny over her actions in connection with the Epstein case, which has drawn significant public and media attention. The decision reflects ongoing frustrations within the administration regarding legal and ethical challenges faced by its officials.

clusters.title: Artemis II Astronauts Launch on Journey to the Moon
clusters.summary: The Artemis II mission has successfully launched, sending a crew of astronauts on a journey toward the moon after a day spent in low Earth orbit. This mission marks a significant step in NASA's efforts to return humans to lunar exploration. The astronauts will conduct various tests and gather data as they approach their destination, aiming to pave the way for future lunar missions.

items.title: Artemis II astronauts approach 100,000 miles from Earth on voyage to the moon
items.description:
Capsule’s engine fires up for six minutes, putting crew on track to reach farthest distance travelled by humans in space

The four astronauts on the Artemis II mission are approaching 100,000 miles from Earth as they head towards the moon, putting them on track to reach the farthest distance humans have ever travelled into space.

The crew have left Earth’s orbit and fired their engines on Thursday for a “translunar injection”, sending the Orion capsule on its trajectory towards the moon.

Continue reading...

clusters.title: Trump Implements New Tariffs on Pharmaceuticals and Metals
clusters.summary: Former President Donald Trump has announced new tariffs on pharmaceuticals and adjustments to existing duties on steel and other metals. This move comes a year after what he termed 'Liberation Day,' and aims to address trade imbalances and support domestic manufacturing. The decision has sparked varied reactions regarding its potential economic impact and implications for international trade relations.

clusters.title: Supreme Court Hears Arguments on Birthright Citizenship Case
clusters.summary: The Supreme Court recently heard oral arguments regarding the contentious issue of birthright citizenship, a topic that has significant implications for immigration policy. Former President Trump indicated his potential attendance at the proceedings, which have drawn attention from various political factions. The outcome of this case could reshape the legal landscape surrounding citizenship rights in the United States.

clusters.title: Cuba to Pardon Over 2,000 Prisoners During Holy Week
clusters.summary: Cuba has announced plans to pardon 2,010 prisoners as a humanitarian gesture coinciding with Holy Week. This decision comes amid ongoing discussions with the United States, although the government has not confirmed a direct link between the pardons and these negotiations. The move is seen as an effort to alleviate domestic tensions and improve international relations.

items.title: Cuba to pardon more than 2,000 prisoners as US eases fuel blockade
items.description:
Havana calls releases a Holy Week humanitarian gesture as Russian tanker is allowed to reach oil-starved island

Cuba has announced plans to pardon 2,010 prisoners as a “humanitarian” gesture during Holy Week, amid heightened US pressure on the government in Havana.

The announcement on Thursday came days after Donald Trump eased a de facto oil blockade of Cuba by allowing a Russian tanker to deliver crude oil to the nation.

Continue reading...

items.title: Cuba Says It Is Pardoning More Than 2,000 Prisoners
items.description: The Cuban government said the releases were a humanitarian gesture during Holy Week. It was not clear if they were related to ongoing negotiations between Cuba and the United States.

items.description: Releasing political prisoners in Cuba has long been a US core demand as oil blockade continues.

items.title: Cuba to release more than 2,000 prisoners as US pressure mounts

clusters.title: Trump Proposes $1.5T Defense Budget with Domestic Cuts
clusters.summary: President Trump has proposed a budget that allocates $1.5 trillion for defense spending while suggesting significant cuts to domestic programs. The proposal is set to be reviewed by Congress, where it is expected to face scrutiny from both sides of the political spectrum.

clusters.title: U.S. Economy Adds 178,000 Jobs in March Amidst Global Tensions
clusters.summary: The U.S. economy added 178,000 jobs in March, surpassing expectations, according to the Labor Department. The unemployment rate remains steady at 4.3%, indicating continued labor market strength despite ongoing global challenges, including tensions related to the conflict in Iran. This job growth reflects resilience in the economy as it navigates various external pressures.

clusters.title: Federal Government Sues States Over Prediction Market Regulations
clusters.summary: The federal government has filed a lawsuit against Arizona, Connecticut, and Illinois, contesting their regulations on prediction market operators, including companies like Kalshi and Polymarket. This legal action raises questions about the balance of state and federal authority in overseeing emerging financial markets. The outcome could significantly impact how prediction markets are regulated across the United States.

clusters.title: Amazon Imposes 3.5% Surcharge on Third-Party Sellers Amid Fuel Spike
clusters.summary: Amazon will implement a 3.5% surcharge on third-party sellers starting later this month, citing increased fuel and logistics costs driven by the ongoing conflict in Iran. This decision reflects the company's response to rising energy prices, which are impacting operational expenses. The surcharge is expected to affect sellers' pricing strategies and could influence consumer costs on the platform.

clusters.title: Myanmar's Coup Leader Min Aung Hlaing Elected President
clusters.summary: General Min Aung Hlaing, the leader of Myanmar's military junta, has been elected president by a pro-military parliament, securing 429 out of 584 votes. This election follows a coup that has led to ongoing civil unrest and violence in the country. The appointment raises concerns about the future of democracy and stability in Myanmar amid a backdrop of significant opposition and conflict.

clusters.title: House Republicans Push for ICE Funding Amid DHS Bill Negotiations
clusters.summary: House Republicans are advocating for funding for Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) before agreeing to a Senate bill aimed at funding the Department of Homeland Security (DHS). This move complicates efforts to prevent a government shutdown, as the Senate has already advanced its proposal that includes funding for the Transportation Security Administration (TSA). The situation highlights the ongoing tensions between the two chambers regarding immigration and security funding.

clusters.title: 14-Year-Old Boy Shot Dead in Woolwich; Three Arrested
clusters.summary: A 14-year-old boy was fatally shot in Woolwich, south-east London, prompting a murder investigation by local police. Three suspects, including two teenagers aged 14 and 16, as well as an 18-year-old man, have been arrested on suspicion of murder. The incident has raised concerns about youth violence in the area.

items.title: Three teenagers arrested after 14-year-old boy shot dead in south-east London
items.description:
Boys aged 14 and 16 and 18-year-old man arrested on suspicion of murder after shooting in Woolwich

Police have launched a murder investigation after a 14-year-old boy was fatally shot in Woolwich, south-east London. Three teenagers have been arrested on suspicion of murder.

Officers received reports of a shooting on Lord Warwick Street, Woolwich, at about 3.40pm on Thursday, the Metropolitan police said.

Continue reading...

items.title: Boy, 14, shot dead and teens arrested for murder
items.description: Two teenagers and one man are arrested after a boy, 14, was shot dead in Woolwich on Thursday.
